issue_title: Page size for darwin facts is inaccurate for Apple Silicon devices
issue_body:
### Summary

Currently the Darwin hardware fact collector references a static 4096 byte value: https://github.com/ansible/ansible/blob/devel/lib/ansible/module_utils/facts/hardware/darwin.py#L96

However, on Apple Silicon this is inaccurate - the page size on apple silicon can be 16384 bytes. E.g, checking against `sysctl` on an M1 macbook pro:

```
sysctl hw.pagesize
hw.pagesize: 16384
```

This is seen in the test fixtures as well:

* https://github.com/ansible/ansible/blob/devel/test/units/module_utils/facts/hardware/fixtures/vm_stat_darwin.txt
* https://github.com/ansible/ansible/blob/devel/test/units/module_utils/facts/hardware/fixtures/sysctl_darwin.txt#L87

As a result, the memory facts for macOS are quite inaccurate. The test fixture above is just self-referential so is currently incorrect as well. 

Should we be calling `self.sysctl['hw.pagesize']` instead to initialise this value?

### Issue Type

Bug Report

### Component Name

facts
